proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of feature_057 (a 3-level ordinal variable, 0/1/2) are associated with lower probability of objective_response (negative direction).
Higher values of feature_011 (continuous, range 0-23.6) are associated with lower probability of objective_response (negative direction).
Higher values of feature_006 (continuous, range 20-100, plausibly a performance/functional score) are associated with lower probability of objective_response (negative direction).

analyses.code:
from scipy import stats
import pandas as pd
ct = pd.crosstab(df['feature_057'], df['objective_response'])
stats.chi2_contingency(ct)
analyses.result_summary: Response rate is monotonically lower across feature_057 levels: 21.3% at 0 (n=17592), 15.5% at 1 (n=24971), 11.2% at 2 (n=7437). Cochran-Armitage style chi-square = 447.9, p = 5.5e-98. Negative ordinal trend confirmed.
Mean feature_011 is 3.38 in responders vs 3.95 in non-responders (Welch t=-13.2, p=7.2e-39). Logistic OR per unit = 0.961, p=1.4e-34. Higher feature_011 reduces response probability.

Mean feature_006 is 58.2 in responders vs 60.3 in non-responders (Welch t=-9.1, p=9.5e-20). Logistic OR per unit = 0.994, p=7.6e-20. Direction is negative — counter-intuitive if feature_006 represents 'performance status' (higher KPS usually predicts better response). The numerical sign is negative on the natural scale.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of feature_063 (continuous, right-skewed, range 0.03-300) are associated with lower probability of objective_response (negative direction).
Higher values of feature_099 (continuous, narrow range 1.7-5.5) are associated with HIGHER probability of objective_response (positive direction).
Higher values of feature_092 (continuous, right-skewed, range 0.5-500) are associated with lower probability of objective_response (negative direction).

analyses.code:
from scipy import stats
stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[y==1,'feature_063'], df.loc[y==0,'feature_063'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_063 is 5.65 (responders) vs 6.17 (non-responders); Welch p=4.4e-7. Logistic OR per unit = 0.993, p=1.4e-6. Negative association supported.

analyses.code:
from scipy import stats
stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[y==1,'feature_099'], df.loc[y==0,'feature_099'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_099 is 3.825 (responders) vs 3.796 (non-responders); Welch p=1.3e-6. Logistic OR per unit = 1.125, p=1.0e-6. Positive association confirmed; effect size small but precise.

analyses.code:
from scipy import stats
stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[y==1,'feature_092'], df.loc[y==0,'feature_092'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_092 is 19.07 (responders) vs 20.40 (non-responders); Welch p=1.3e-5. Logistic OR per unit = 0.998, p=9.6e-6. Negative association supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_035 = 1 have a HIGHER probability of objective_response than feature_035 = 0 (positive direction).
Patients with feature_093 = 1 have a HIGHER probability of objective_response than feature_093 = 0 (positive direction).
Patients with feature_121 = 1 have a LOWER probability of objective_response than feature_121 = 0 (negative direction).

analyses.result_summary: Response rate 22.1% (feature_035=1, n=3624) vs 16.5% (feature_035=0, n=46376). Chi-square OR=1.439, p=3.5e-18. Strong positive association.
Response rate 18.5% (feature_093=1, n=4025) vs 16.8% (feature_093=0, n=45975). OR=1.126, p=5.6e-3. Modest positive association.
Response rate 15.6% (feature_121=1, n=4143) vs 17.0% (feature_121=0, n=45857). OR=0.904, p=2.5e-2. Modest negative association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_014 = 1 have a HIGHER probability of objective_response than feature_014 = 0 (positive direction).
Patients with feature_012 = 1 have a LOWER probability of objective_response than feature_012 = 0 (negative direction).
Patients with feature_111 = 1 have a LOWER probability of objective_response than feature_111 = 0 (negative direction).

analyses.result_summary: Response rate 17.3% (feature_014=1, n=20020) vs 16.6% (feature_014=0, n=29980). OR=1.054, chi-square p=3.2e-2. Modest positive effect.
Response rate 16.5% (feature_012=1, n=20997) vs 17.2% (feature_012=0, n=29003). OR=0.953, p=5.1e-2. Borderline negative; not significant at 0.05.
Response rate 15.9% (feature_111=1, n=4983) vs 17.0% (feature_111=0, n=45017). OR=0.925, p=5.7e-2. Borderline negative; not significant at 0.05.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Response rate differs across categories of feature_005 (race/ethnicity: white, black, hispanic, asian, other).
Response rate differs across categories of feature_087 (insurance: medicare, medicaid, private, uninsured).
Each unit increase in feature_057 reduces the log-odds of response in a linear/dose-response manner (refining h1).

analyses.result_summary: Response rates: white 17.3%, hispanic 16.7%, asian 16.3%, black 15.7%, other 14.7%. 4-df chi-square = 13.4, p = 9.6e-3. Significant heterogeneity; whites highest, 'other' lowest.
Response rates: medicare 17.2%, medicaid 16.9%, private 16.7%, uninsured 16.6%. 3-df chi-square = 2.16, p = 0.54. No significant variation by insurance type.

analyses.code:
import statsmodels.api as sm
X = sm.add_constant(df['feature_057']); m = sm.Logit(y, X).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Logistic regression with feature_057 modeled as ordinal: coefficient = -0.392 per unit, OR = 0.676 per unit (each step lowers odds of response by ~32%), z=-21.3, p=4e-100. Linear dose-response holds.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Response rate varies across the 5 levels of feature_096 (0-4).
Response rate varies across the 11 levels of feature_018 (0-10).
Response rate varies across the 5 levels of feature_125 (0-4).

analyses.result_summary: Response rates 16.4-17.7% across levels 0-4 of feature_096; 4-df chi-square = 6.20, p = 0.18. No significant variation.
Response rates 16.3-19.0% across levels 0-10 of feature_018; 10-df chi-square = 6.88, p = 0.74. No significant variation.
Response rates 15.7-17.4% across levels 0-4 of feature_125; 4-df chi-square = 8.55, p = 0.073. Borderline; weak negative trend (16.6 -> 15.7 from low to high) but not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for top features (feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, feature_099, feature_092, feature_035, feature_093, feature_121, feature_014, race), the negative association between feature_057 and objective_response remains the strongest single predictor.
After multivariable adjustment, the positive feature_035 effect remains independently significant.

analyses.code: X = pd.concat([... top features, race dummies], 1); m = sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant(X)).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logistic model (n=50000, pseudo-R^2 = 0.019). Independently significant: feature_057 coef=-0.384, p=8.6e-97; feature_011 coef=-0.040, p=1.4e-34; feature_006 coef=-0.0057, p=7.6e-20; feature_035 coef=+0.360, p=1.8e-17 (OR=1.43); feature_099 coef=+0.118, p=1.0e-6; feature_063 coef=-0.0073, p=1.4e-6; feature_092 coef=-0.0022, p=9.6e-6; feature_093 coef=+0.126, p=3.3e-3; race_black coef=-0.109, p=5.1e-3; race_other coef=-0.197, p=3.0e-2. feature_057 is the strongest single predictor on the log-odds scale; feature_035 remains highly significant after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_011 on objective_response is modified by feature_057 (interaction term significant).
The effect of feature_035 on objective_response is modified by feature_057 (interaction term significant).

analyses.result_summary: feature_011 x feature_057 interaction: coef=-0.002, p=0.71. No effect modification — feature_011 acts uniformly across feature_057 strata.
feature_035 x feature_057 interaction: coef=+0.027, p=0.67. The benefit of feature_035 is statistically homogeneous across feature_057 strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive multiplicative interaction between feature_035 and feature_014 — the response benefit of feature_035 is amplified when feature_014 = 1.
There is a positive multiplicative interaction between feature_093 and feature_121.

analyses.result_summary: feature_035 x feature_014 interaction coefficient = +0.730, p = 7.7e-18. Cell response rates: (035=0,014=0)=16.6%, (035=0,014=1)=16.4%, (035=1,014=0)=17.1%, (035=1,014=1)=29.8%. Within feature_014=1 stratum, feature_035 OR=2.16, p=8.9e-38; within feature_014=0 stratum, feature_035 OR=1.04, p=0.51. Strong, replicated synergistic interaction: feature_035 benefit is essentially confined to feature_014=1 patients.

feature_093 x feature_121 interaction coefficient = +0.421, p = 3.7e-3. Cell response rates: (093=0,121=0)=16.9%, (093=0,121=1)=15.0%, (093=1,121=0)=18.1%, (093=1,121=1)=22.6%. Modest synergistic positive interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a significant interaction between feature_006 and feature_092 such that the joint effect on response differs from additivity on the log-odds scale.
There is a significant interaction between feature_011 and feature_063 (two top continuous predictors).
There is a significant interaction between feature_011 and feature_006.

analyses.result_summary: feature_006 x feature_092 interaction: coef = -7.2e-5 per unit-product, p = 5.6e-3. Both main effects negative; the negative interaction means that high feature_006 + high feature_092 jointly suppress response more than their additive effect.
feature_011 x feature_063 interaction: coef = -7.3e-4, p = 0.092. Not significant at 0.05.
feature_011 x feature_006 interaction: coef = +2.9e-4, p = 0.093. Not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Response rate varies in a graded (monotonic) manner across quartiles of feature_011, with Q4 (highest) showing the lowest response.
Response rate varies in a graded manner across quartiles of feature_006, with the highest quartile showing the lowest response.

analyses.result_summary: feature_011 dose-response (3 effective bins due to ties at 0): low (<=3.0) 18.6% (n=25138), middle (3.0-6.4) 16.8% (n=12562), high (>6.4) 13.6% (n=12300). Trend chi-square = 145.4, p = 2.6e-32. Monotonic negative trend confirmed.
feature_006 quartiles: Q1 (20-46.6) 19.0%, Q2 (46.6-60) 17.5%, Q3 (60-73.3) 16.5%, Q4 (73.3-100) 14.7%. Trend chi-square = 86.7, p = 1.1e-18. Monotonic negative gradient.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_011 > 6.4 (top 25% of distribution) have a clinically/statistically lower response rate than the bottom 75%.
log(feature_011 + 1) is a more concentrated predictor of response than the raw scale (negative direction preserved).

analyses.result_summary: Response rate 13.6% in feature_011>6.4 (n=12300) vs 18.0% in feature_011<=6.4 (n=37700). OR=0.717, chi-square p=1.9e-29. Strong negative effect at threshold.

Mean log(feature_011+1) is 1.105 (responders) vs 1.226 (non-responders), Welch t=-11.2, p=3.9e-29. Negative direction preserved on log scale (slightly less peaked than raw t-test, suggesting effect is dispersed, not solely from extreme values).

proposed_hypotheses.text: log(feature_092 + 1) is significantly lower in responders, preserving the negative direction seen on the raw scale and indicating the effect is robust to skew.
Categorizing feature_006 as <=50 / 51-70 / >70 produces a graded negative gradient consistent with a performance-status interpretation.

analyses.result_summary: Mean log(feature_092+1) is 2.570 (responders) vs 2.633 (non-responders), Welch t=-5.9, p=3.4e-9. Negative effect robust to log transform.
Categorical feature_006: <=50 -> 18.9%, 51-70 -> 16.9%, >70 -> 14.9%. 2-df chi-square = 86.9, p = 1.3e-19. Graded negative trend consistent with quartile analysis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: White patients have a higher response rate than non-white patients (univariate).
Black patients have a lower response rate than non-black patients (univariate).

analyses.result_summary: Response rate 17.3% in white (n=32498) vs 16.2% in non-white (n=17502). OR=1.081, chi-square p=2.1e-3. Small but significant positive effect for white vs non-white.
Response rate 15.7% in black (n=5889) vs 17.1% in non-black (n=44111). OR=0.908, chi-square p=1.2e-2. Small but significant negative effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment for feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, feature_099, feature_092, feature_035, feature_014, the residual race_black effect is negative and remains significant.
After multivariable adjustment, the race_other effect is negative and significant; race_hispanic and race_asian are not significantly different from white.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted log-odds (vs white): race_black coef=-0.109, OR=0.897, p=5.5e-3; race_other coef=-0.197, OR=0.821, p=3.0e-2; race_hispanic coef=-0.035, p=0.31 (NS); race_asian coef=-0.079, p=0.13 (NS). Black and 'other' patients have ~10-18% lower odds of response than white patients with otherwise comparable features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within feature_057=0 (lowest stratum), white patients still have a higher response rate than black patients.
Within feature_057=2 (highest stratum), the response rate gap by race persists.

analyses.result_summary: feature_057=0 stratum (n=17592): white 21.7%, hispanic 20.9%, black 20.5%, asian 20.4%, other 17.0%. Direction preserved: white slightly higher; absolute differences modest in this stratum (a few percentage points). No formal interaction — gap homogeneous across feature_057.

feature_057=2 stratum (n=7437): white 11.7%, hispanic 10.9%, asian 10.5%, black 9.5%, other 8.6%. Same ordering with smaller absolute response rates. The white-vs-non-white gap in odds is approximately preserved (interaction with feature_057 NS).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Insurance type (feature_087) has no association with response after stratifying by feature_057, indicating the apparent univariate null is not confounded.

analyses.result_summary: Response rates by feature_087 within each feature_057 stratum are essentially identical (within 1 percentage point). E.g., at feature_057=1: medicaid 15.2%, medicare 15.8%, private 15.5%, uninsured 14.2%. Confirms feature_087 has no main or stratum-specific effect on response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between feature_121 and feature_011 — the negative effect of feature_011 is steeper among feature_121=1 patients.
There is a negative interaction between feature_035 and feature_099 — the positive effect of feature_099 is attenuated among feature_035=1 patients.
There is a positive interaction between feature_121 and feature_099.

analyses.result_summary: feature_121 x feature_011 interaction coef = -0.034, p = 7.7e-3. Within feature_121=1 patients, the negative slope of feature_011 is steeper than in feature_121=0 patients.
feature_035 x feature_099 interaction coef = -0.187, p = 2.4e-2. The positive feature_099 slope is somewhat dampened when feature_035 = 1.
feature_121 x feature_099 interaction coef = +0.222, p = 1.3e-2. Among feature_121=1 patients, the positive feature_099 slope is steeper, partially offsetting the main negative effect of feature_121.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After including the feature_035 x feature_014 interaction, the main effect of feature_035 alone (when feature_014=0) is no longer significant — i.e., the apparent main effect was driven by the interaction.

analyses.code: Multivariable logit with feature_035, feature_014, and feature_035*feature_014
analyses.result_summary: Joint model: feature_035 main effect coef = +0.024, p=0.69 (NS, this represents feature_035 effect when feature_014=0); feature_014 main coef = -0.010, p=0.69 (NS); interaction coef = +0.763, p = 5.5e-19. Confirms the feature_035 benefit is essentially confined to feature_014=1 patients; among feature_014=0 patients there is no detectable feature_035 effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within feature_014=1 patients, feature_035 doubles the odds of response (OR ~ 2); within feature_014=0 patients, feature_035 has no effect.

analyses.code: Stratified chi-square within each feature_014 level
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_014=1 (n=20020): feature_035=1 response 29.8% (n=1435) vs feature_035=0 16.4% (n=18585), OR=2.16, chi-square p=8.9e-38. Within feature_014=0 (n=29980): feature_035=1 response 17.1% (n=2189) vs feature_035=0 16.6% (n=27791), OR=1.04, p=0.51. Strongly supports the hypothesis: feature_035 confers a large benefit only within feature_014=1 patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 modeled as continuous in the adjusted model has a per-5-unit OR of ~0.82 (substantively meaningful effect on the natural scale).

analyses.code: exp(5 * coef_feature_011) from the multivariable model
analyses.result_summary: Per-unit adjusted log-OR = -0.040; exp(5 x -0.040) = 0.819. A 5-unit rise in feature_011 corresponds to ~18% lower odds of response. p<1e-30 in adjusted model.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006 dichotomized at 70 (a common KPS-like cutoff) shows a >70 vs <=70 OR significantly below 1.

analyses.result_summary: Computed via 3-bin categorization above: response 14.9% in feature_006>70 vs 17.9% in feature_006<=70 (combining first two bins). OR ~0.80, chi-square p < 1e-15. Direction confirmed: high feature_006 (>70) is associated with lower response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Outliers in feature_063 (max=300) are not driving its negative association with response — winsorizing at the 99th percentile preserves the direction and significance.

analyses.code: winz = np.minimum(df['feature_063'], df['feature_063'].quantile(0.99)); stats.ttest_ind(winz[y==1], winz[y==0], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Winsorized feature_063 at 99th pct (42.2): mean(resp) ~5.5, mean(noresp) ~6.0, t-test p < 1e-6. Direction and significance robust to extreme values.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within white patients (the largest subgroup), feature_057 retains its monotonic negative dose-response on objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Within white subgroup: feature_057=0 -> 21.7% (n=11401), =1 -> 15.8% (n=16270), =2 -> 11.7% (n=4827). Monotonic negative gradient preserved. Cochran-Armitage type test p < 1e-50.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A parsimonious final logistic model containing feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_035, feature_014, feature_035 x feature_014, feature_099, feature_063, feature_092, race captures all the major signals identified in iterations 1-24, with feature_057 as the dominant predictor and the feature_035 x feature_014 interaction as the most clinically actionable subgroup effect.

analyses.code: Final multivariable logistic regression with interaction term
analyses.result_summary: Final adjusted model: feature_057 OR=0.680 (p=2e-97); feature_011 OR=0.961 per unit (p=8e-35); feature_006 OR=0.994 per unit (p=3e-20); feature_035 main OR=1.02 NS; feature_014 main OR=0.99 NS; feature_035*feature_014 OR=2.14 (p=5e-19); feature_099 OR=1.13 per unit (p=8e-7); feature_063 OR=0.993 per unit (p=1.4e-6); feature_092 OR=0.998 per unit (p=1.1e-5); race_black OR=0.90 (p=5e-3), race_other OR=0.82 (p=3e-2). Pseudo-R^2 ~ 0.020. The dominant signals are: feature_057 (3-level ordinal, perhaps a stage/risk score), feature_011 (continuous, top 25% have ~28% lower odds), feature_006 (continuous, perhaps performance status — note negative direction), and the synergistic feature_035*feature_014 interaction (a positive predictive marker only useful in feature_014-positive patients). Race effects are small, residually significant for black and 'other' vs white.
